# One-pager: Pick up where you left off
Authors: Jeremy Kent | Last updated: Jun 1, 2026 | Under review

## Motivation / Problem
About 25% of patients who complete Guided Matching subsequently visit a fresh landing
page, and about 40% of those go on to start Guided Matching a second time. This repetitive
friction for return visitors likely drives preventable drop-off.

## How we will solve it
Cache search criteria via local storage or cookies; provide a landing page affordance for
returning patients on the same device to jump straight back to their results.

## Success criteria
+1-3% S2R, S2P. ~5-10% rel. lift for the 25% who return to a second landing page.

## Confidence
6/10

## Interview answers (recorded 2026-06-05)
- Experimentation: likely an A/B test, but treated as Medium (2 wks), no validation UXR needed.
- Component: new component.
- Surface owner: product-owned (no cross-team dependency).
- Solution space: a few directions to explore (banner vs modal vs auto-restore).
